summary: Scan each BoardSmith game to determine which audit-fix changes will force that game to change its implementation

games.overall: Moderately affected. The single biggest required change is F23+F28 (selection-builder consolidation): the move action's second step uses `.chooseFrom<DestinationChoice>('destination', ...)` with a custom value-object encoding (pieceId/notation/isCapture/becomesKing) plus `filterBy`, and BOTH the rules `execute()` and the custom UI `executeMove()` read/produce that object shape rather than an element ID. If `chooseFrom` is removed in favor of `chooseElement`/`chooseElements` with element-ID encoding, this action and the custom board component must be rewritten. Everything else is light: imports are already canonical, rules code is sandbox-clean, no array/object/Map/Set state, and no server/debug coupling. Secondary risks are F22 (board highlight/selection are matched by notation-only while action refs carry ids — precedence change could break panel↔board sync) and a couple of src/ui determinism patterns (Date.now/setTimeout) that an expanded validate scan would flag.
games.usesBoardsmithHow: Consumes BoardSmith via the canonical `boardsmith` package as a local file dependency (package.json: "boardsmith": "file:../../BoardSmith"). Imports use canonical entry points only: 'boardsmith', 'boardsmith/ui', 'boardsmith/ai', 'boardsmith/testing'. No @boardsmith/* paths, no vendored copy, no vite alias (vite.config.ts is bare with only the Vue plugin). Ships a custom UI (App.vue + CheckersBoard.vue inside GameShell's #game-board slot) and custom AI objectives, but no custom server/client — relies on the CLI dev server (`npx boardsmith dev`) and GameShell defaults.

impacts.evidence: src/rules/actions.ts:81 `.chooseElement<CheckerPiece>('piece', {...})` (canonical, kept) but src/rules/actions.ts:114 `.chooseFrom<DestinationChoice>('destination', {choices, display, boardRefs, filterBy})`. execute reads it as a non-ID object: actions.ts:173 `const destination = args.destination as DestinationChoice;` then uses `destination.toNotation` (179,239). The custom UI also builds and sends the object: src/ui/components/CheckersBoard.vue:564-575 `const destination = { pieceId, fromNotation, toNotation, isCapture, becomesKing }; await props.actionController.execute('move', { piece: move.pieceId, destination })`.
impacts.requiredChange: Migrate the 'destination' selection off chooseFrom. Either re-express it as a Square chooseElement (element-ID encoding) and recompute capture/king metadata inside execute() from piece+target, or keep a value selection if chooseFrom survives. Update CheckersBoard.vue executeMove to send element IDs instead of the DestinationChoice object. filterBy{key:'pieceId',selectionName:'piece'} also needs an ID-based equivalent.

impacts.evidence: grep of all imports: src/ui/App.vue:2 and CheckersBoard.vue:16 'boardsmith/ui'; src/rules/*.ts 'boardsmith' and 'boardsmith/ai' (ai.ts:2); tests/game.test.ts:8 'boardsmith/testing'. No '@boardsmith/*' anywhere. package.json dep is "boardsmith": "file:../../BoardSmith". vite.config.ts has no alias; no vendor dir.

src/rules is clean (grep for Math.random/Date.now/new Date/fetch/WebSocket/setTimeout/eval/Function/crypto/process/require over src/rules returned nothing; only Math.abs used in game.ts/ai.ts). src/ui hits: CheckersBoard.vue:187 `id: `capture-fly-${pieceId}-${Date.now()}`` and CheckersBoard.vue:583 `setTimeout(() => { isMoving.value = false; }, 300)`.
impacts.requiredChange: If 'boardsmith validate' is expanded to scan all of src/ (not just src/rules) or eslint sandbox rules are wired in, these two src/ui patterns would be flagged. They are legitimate UI concerns (unique animation id, animation debounce); needs allowance for src/ui or a non-Date.now id source. No rules-code change required.

impacts.evidence: CheckersGame declares only element refs and a boolean: src/rules/game.ts:36-40 `continuingPlayer: CheckersPlayer | null`, `continuingPiece: CheckerPiece | null`, `hasMovedThisTurn: boolean`. Element fields are scalars: src/rules/elements.ts isKing:boolean (9), row/col/isDark, CheckersPlayer.capturedCount:number (121). No plain array/object mutable state declared.

The only class-instance state is element refs (game.ts:36-37 continuingPlayer/continuingPiece). The engine's serializeValue special-cases these before any throw: src/engine/element/game-element.ts:753 `if (value instanceof GameElement)` → returns {__playerRef} / {__elementRef}. No Map/Set/function/circular/deeply-nested plain objects stored in game or element state.

CheckersBoard.vue is rendered inside GameShell's #game-board slot (src/ui/App.vue:61-73), so useBoardInteraction() (CheckersBoard.vue:19) resolves the provided injection and will not throw. The component does defensively guard the result (CheckersBoard.vue:376 `boardInteraction?.clear()`, :397 `if (boardInteraction)`, :433,:440,:447,:454,:580), but those branches simply become dead/unnecessary, not broken.
impacts.requiredChange: Optional cleanup: drop the now-unnecessary `if (boardInteraction)` / `?.` guards. Not required for correctness.

impacts.evidence: Board checks panel state by notation ONLY: CheckersBoard.vue:442 `boardInteraction.isHighlighted({ notation })` and :449 `boardInteraction.isSelected({ notation })`. But the action registers refs that carry id: src/rules/actions.ts:106-111 boardRef `{ id: piece.id, notation }` and :136-146 boardRefs sourceRef/targetRef with `{ id: ..., notation }`. Current matchesRef (src/ui/composables/useBoardInteraction.ts:206-209) ORs id||name||notation; precedence (id wins) means a notation-only element compared against an id-bearing ref would no longer fall through to notation. selectElement also passes both: CheckersBoard.vue:457 `{ id: pieceId, notation }`.
impacts.requiredChange: Confirm exact precedence semantics. If id takes precedence over notation in matchesRef, the board's isHighlighted/isSelected (which pass notation only) will stop matching id-bearing action refs, breaking panel→board highlight/selection sync. Fix by passing the element id in those checks, or having the action emit notation-only refs for squares.

impacts.evidence: src/rules/actions.ts is full of casts that typed args/ctx would make redundant: `ctx.player as CheckersPlayer` (lines 16,22,86,117,156,163,171), `element as CheckerPiece` (85), `game.getElementById(id) as CheckerPiece` (76), and in execute `args.piece as CheckerPiece` (172) and `args.destination as DestinationChoice` (173). No `(ctx.game as ...)` casts (game is captured via closure).
impacts.requiredChange: Cleanup only: once execute() args are typed from declared selections, the `as CheckerPiece`/`as DestinationChoice` casts are unnecessary; `ctx.player` casts may also tighten. Low risk of new compile errors.

impacts.evidence: No use of boardsmith/client, GameConnection, MeepleClient, or a custom server. UI connects purely through GameShell (src/ui/App.vue:56-60) and the project runs via the CLI dev server (package.json scripts: "dev": "npx boardsmith dev").
grep over src/ and tests/ for rewind/deck-stack/shuffle/full-history//debug/debugConfig returned no matches. No debug/rewind endpoints are consumed.

games.overall: Moderately affected. The two real required changes are: (1) F23+F28 — the discard action uses `.fromElements<Card>('cards', {...})` (a builder slated for removal/aliasing) and must migrate to `chooseElements`; and (2) F3 — the game deliberately persists mutable array state through a "settings shadow" (game.settings._currentPlayCards / _playerSaidGo and per-player _originalHandCardIds) plus two declared deep plain-object properties (scoringAnimation, roundSummary), exactly the persistence pattern the audit is reworking, so this state mechanism will likely need to change. Everything else is clean or cosmetic: all imports already use canonical `boardsmith*` paths, no custom server/client, no useBoardInteraction, rules code is deterministic (uses this.random()). The single biggest required change is reworking the settings-shadow array persistence (F3); the fromElements migration (F23) is the most clearly breaking but mechanically small.
games.usesBoardsmithHow: Consumes BoardSmith via the canonical package name only: package.json declares "boardsmith": "file:../../BoardSmith" and all source imports use 'boardsmith', 'boardsmith/ui', 'boardsmith/ai', 'boardsmith/testing'. No @boardsmith/* paths, no vendored copy, no vite alias (vite.config.ts only adds the vue plugin). Ships a custom UI (src/ui/components/CribbageBoard.vue + RoundSummary.vue) rendered inside GameShell/AutoUI; main.ts just mounts App.vue. No custom server/client wiring — relies on the CLI dev server (npx boardsmith dev) and GameShell defaults.

impacts.evidence: src/rules/actions.ts:17 `.fromElements<Card>('cards', { ... multiSelect: { min: 2, max: 2 } })` for the discard action (initial grep missed it due to the `<Card>` type param). src/rules/actions.ts:68 `.chooseElement('card', {...})` is the canonical kept form. Selection encoding is already element IDs: UI sends `{ cards: selectedCards.value }` (CribbageBoard.vue:520-522) and `{ card: cardId }` (CribbageBoard.vue:572-573), and execute reads `args.cards as Card[]` (actions.ts:42).
impacts.requiredChange: Rename the discard builder from `.fromElements('cards', ...)` to the canonical `.chooseElements('cards', ...)`, keeping the multiSelect/min-max config. No value-encoding change needed (already IDs). chooseElement('card') stays as-is.

impacts.evidence: rg of all imports: src/ui/App.vue:2 'boardsmith/ui'; src/rules/actions.ts:1 'boardsmith'; src/ui/components/CribbageBoard.vue:17 'boardsmith/ui'; src/rules/flow.ts/game.ts/elements.ts 'boardsmith'; src/rules/ai.ts:2 'boardsmith/ai'; tests/game.test.ts:8 'boardsmith/testing'. package.json: "boardsmith": "file:../../BoardSmith". No '@boardsmith/...' imports, no vendoring/alias.

src/rules/** is clean of forbidden host APIs — randomness uses the engine RNG (game.ts:193 `this.random() < 0.5`); grep for Math.random/Date.now/fetch/setTimeout/etc in src/rules returned nothing. src/ui hits: src/ui/components/RoundSummary.vue:58,89,117,124,129,152 use setTimeout for reveal animations; src/ui/components/CribbageBoard.vue:538 `id: \`discard-${card.id}-${Date.now()}\``. These are UI-only.
impacts.requiredChange: Rules code already compliant — no change. If 'boardsmith validate' is expanded to scan all of src/ and the lint rules treat setTimeout/Date.now as violations even in UI, the UI animation timers and the Date.now-based fly key would need allowlisting or replacement (e.g. a monotonic counter / crypto-free unique key). Confirm whether the expanded scan exempts src/ui.

impacts.evidence: Game subclass stores mutable array state via a settings shadow: src/rules/game.ts:67-72 `get/set currentPlayCards` -> `this.settings._currentPlayCards as string[]`; game.ts:75-80 `get/set playerSaidGo` -> `this.settings._playerSaidGo`. Element does the same: src/rules/elements.ts:86-95 `originalHandCardIds` -> `this.game.settings[\`_originalHandCardIds_${this.seat}\`]`. Comment at game.ts:64 'HMR-safe state (stored in settings)' shows this is a deliberate workaround for array persistence. Also two declared deep plain-object props holding mutable round state: game.ts:88-114 `scoringAnimation` and game.ts:116-148 `roundSummary` (reassigned wholesale, but nested arrays).
impacts.requiredChange: If the audit changes how declared array/object state persists (away from a settings shadow toward serializable shapes / element children), migrate currentPlayCards, playerSaidGo, and originalHandCardIds off `this.settings`/`this.game.settings` to whatever the new sanctioned mechanism is (plain declared serializable properties or element-backed state). Verify scoringAnimation/roundSummary still persist across save/HMR without the dev-only Proxy.

impacts.evidence: All stored game/element state is JSON-serializable: numbers (runningTotal, score, dealerPosition...), strings, string[]/boolean[] arrays, and plain nested objects (scoringAnimation game.ts:88-114, roundSummary game.ts:116-148). getDiscardCounts (game.ts:848-857) returns a Map but it is a transient method return, not stored state. No Map/Set/class-instance/circular value is persisted in game or element fields. The `import type { CribbageGame }` at elements.ts:99 is type-only (no runtime cycle in serialized data).

grep for useBoardInteraction/injectBoardInteraction in src/ui returned no hits. The custom UI receives an actionController via props (CribbageBoard.vue uses `props.actionController.execute(...)` e.g. :520, :572) and is rendered inside GameShell/AutoUI (App.vue:2). No reliance on a board-interaction undefined fallback.

No board-interaction highlight/select-by-name/notation calls in src/ui (grep for highlight/selectElement/notation/byName empty). Element references use ids: actions.ts:26 `boardRef: (card) => ({ id: card.id })`; UI dispatches element ids (CribbageBoard.vue:521,573). DOM lookups use data-zone attributes (querySelector('[data-zone="crib"]'), CribbageBoard.vue:531,540), not engine ref matching.

src/rules/actions.ts is dense with casts that become unnecessary under generic ActionContext<G> and typed execute args: e.g. `const game = ctx.game as CribbageGame` (lines 20,33,39,72,77,89,95,151,192,197), `ctx.player as CribbagePlayer` (21,34,40,...), and arg casts `args.cards as Card[]` (42), `args.card as Card` (98). Condition predicates also use `(ctx.game as CribbageGame)` (31,87,138,190).
impacts.requiredChange: Optional cleanup: drop the now-redundant `ctx.game as CribbageGame` / `ctx.player` / `args.* as` casts once context and selection args are generically typed. Mostly additive; watch for any cast that no longer matches the inferred type and may newly error.

impacts.evidence: No custom connection wiring: grep for GameConnection/MeepleClient/boardsmith/client/boardsmith/server returned nothing. src/main.ts just `createApp(App).mount('#app')`; networking is handled by the CLI dev server (package.json scripts: "dev": "npx boardsmith dev") and GameShell defaults.

No debug/rewind/deck-stack/shuffle/history client calls. The only 'shuffle' is the engine method game.ts:271 `this.deck.shuffle()` (game rule, not a debug endpoint). The 'debug' grep hits are console.log statements in CribbageBoard.vue and comments in tests/game.test.ts (381,384,390,410), not debug-endpoint usage.

games.overall: This is a small single-file-rules demo whose entire purpose is to showcase ActionPanel selection builders, so it is heavily affected by the selection-builder consolidation (F23+F28) — that is the single biggest required change. The game imports BoardSmith via the canonical 'boardsmith' / 'boardsmith/ui' entrypoints, ships no custom server/client, has no sandbox violations, no board-interaction code, and no tests, so every other audit item is unaffected or only minor cleanup. The dominant work is migrating 5 .chooseFrom() value-selection sites plus reconciling the chooseElement result encoding if selection results unify to element IDs.
games.usesBoardsmithHow: Consumes BoardSmith as a normal file: dependency ("boardsmith": "file:../../BoardSmith") and imports only the canonical package entrypoints: 'boardsmith' (engine: Action, Game, Player, Piece, Space, loop, actionStep, types) in src/rules, and 'boardsmith/ui' (GameShell, AutoUI) in src/ui/App.vue. No vendoring, no vite alias (vite.config.ts only adds the vue plugin), no @boardsmith/* scoped imports. Runs via the CLI dev server (scripts call `npx boardsmith dev/build/validate`); UI is just GameShell + AutoUI defaults. No custom server/client wiring.

impacts.evidence: src/rules/actions.ts:31 `.chooseFrom<string>('multiSelect', {`, :104 `.chooseFrom<string>('optional', {`, :127 `.chooseFrom<string>('disabled', {`, :193 `.chooseFrom<string>('repeatUntil', {`, :220 `.chooseFrom('player', {` — five chooseFrom sites. Also :21 `.chooseElement<Unit>('chooseElement', {` whose result is consumed as a live element instance: :57 `const unit = args.chooseElement as Unit; ... unit.hasAttacked = true` and :40 `const unit = ctx.args.chooseElement as Unit`. Selection results are read as non-ID encodings: :73 enemies matched by name (`args.multiSelect` strings) and :227 `const seat = args.player as number`.
impacts.requiredChange: If chooseFrom is removed/aliased, all 5 call sites must migrate to the surviving value-selection API (these select strings/player-seats, not elements, so they need whatever replaces value choices). Separately, if selection encoding unifies to element IDs, the chooseElement consumer must resolve the ID to a Unit instead of casting args.chooseElement directly to a live element, and the value-selection consumers (enemy names, player seat) must be checked against the new encoding.

impacts.evidence: rg over src/ shows only canonical entrypoints: src/ui/App.vue:2 `from 'boardsmith/ui'`; src/rules/actions.ts:1, flow.ts:5, elements.ts:1, game.ts:1 all `from 'boardsmith'`. No '@boardsmith/*' imports. package.json dependency is `"boardsmith": "file:../../BoardSmith"`. No vendor dir or vite alias (vite.config.ts only registers the vue plugin).

Grep of src/rules and src/ui for Math.random|Date.now|new Date|fetch|XMLHttpRequest|WebSocket|setTimeout|setInterval|eval|new Function|crypto|process.|require( produced only one hit, src/rules/actions.ts:33, which is a comment `// dependsOn: re-evaluate choices when chooseElement changes` (the substring 'eval' inside 're-evaluate'), not an eval call. No actual nondeterminism or host access in rules or UI.

Game subclass props (src/rules/game.ts:37-43): `battlefield!: Battlefield` (element), `selectedUnit: Unit | null = null` (element ref, never assigned — grep shows only the declaration at game.ts:40), `turn: number = 1` (primitive). Elements (src/rules/elements.ts) hold only primitives (score, unitName, maxTargets, strength, health, hasAttacked, enemyName, defeated) and `owner!: TestPlayer`. No designer-declared plain array or plain-object mutable-state properties exist.

Only non-primitive state candidates are element references: game.ts:40 `selectedUnit: Unit | null = null` (never assigned, stays null at runtime — grep found no writes) and elements.ts:21 `owner!: TestPlayer`. No Map/Set/function/circular/deeply-nested plain objects stored in game or element state. Element-reference fields are handled by the engine's element serialization, not as raw class instances.

Grep of src/ui for useBoardInteraction/injectBoardInteraction returned nothing. src/ui/App.vue uses only GameShell + AutoUI (App.vue:2,6-12); no board-interaction inject and no optional-chaining on its result.

No board-interaction/highlight/select calls in src/ui (App.vue only renders AutoUI). Elements do define notation getters (elements.ts:38 `get notation()` returns unitName, :56 returns enemyName) but nothing in the UI matches elements by name/notation, so the precedence change cannot collide here.

src/rules/actions.ts uses untyped ActionContext and many arg casts inside execute bodies: :35/:39 `(ctx: ActionContext)`, :40 `ctx.args.chooseElement as Unit`, :56 `ctx.player as TestPlayer`, :57 `args.chooseElement as Unit`, :61-64 `args.multiSelect as string[]` / `as string`, :110 `args.optional as string | undefined`, :199 `args.repeatUntil as string[]`, :227 `args.player as number`. No `(ctx.game as ...)` casts present.
impacts.requiredChange: Mostly additive: once execute args are typed from declared selections, several `as` casts become unnecessary cleanup. Some casts (notably `args.chooseElement as Unit` and the string-based value casts) may newly conflict with the new generated types — needs a compile check, but low effort.

impacts.evidence: No imports of boardsmith/client, no GameConnection/MeepleClient/custom server (grep returned nothing). package.json scripts use `npx boardsmith dev/build/validate`; UI is GameShell defaults only (App.vue). Game relies entirely on the CLI dev server and shell-provided connection.
Grep of src/ (and no tests dir exists) for debug/rewind/shuffle/deck/history found no usage. No debug-config or debug-endpoint calls.

games.overall: This game will be moderately-to-heavily affected, driven almost entirely by F23+F28 (selection-builder consolidation). Every one of its 9 actions uses the soon-to-be-removed .fromElements() (10 call sites total) and one action (flipReorder) uses .chooseFrom() with non-element string choices, which has no element-only replacement and must be redesigned to select a Zone element. The single biggest required change is migrating all element selections from fromElements/chooseFrom to chooseElement, plus reworking flipReorder's string-based zone picker. Everything else is low-risk: imports are already canonical (boardsmith/*), rules code is sandbox-clean (uses engine this.random()), there is no custom server/client, no debug endpoints, no useBoardInteraction, and no array/Map/Set game state. Secondary cleanup: ~18 as-casts in execute bodies (F19+F25) and two UI-only host calls (setTimeout, Date.now) that the expanded validate scan (F13) may newly flag.
games.usesBoardsmithHow: Consumes BoardSmith via the canonical published entry points only: 'boardsmith' (rules), 'boardsmith/ui' (App.vue, GameTable.vue), 'boardsmith/testing' (tests), plus a CSS import 'boardsmith/ui/animation/drag-drop.css'. package.json depends on "boardsmith": "file:../../BoardSmith" (local file link, not vendored, no vite alias). Ships a custom Vue UI (App.vue wrapping GameShell + custom GameTable.vue) but no custom server/client — it relies on the CLI dev server ("npx boardsmith dev") and GameShell defaults.

impacts.evidence: src/rules/actions.ts uses .fromElements at lines 25,59,93,183,219,249,265,319,357 (e.g. line 25 `.fromElements<Card>('card', {`) and .chooseFrom at line 134 `.chooseFrom<string>('zone', {`. No .chooseElement/.chooseElements anywhere. flipReorder (line 134) selects by arbitrary string ('zone-a'..'zone-d') via choices(), not elements, then maps string->Zone in execute (lines 156-167). Execute bodies read resolved elements, e.g. line 44 `const card = args.card as Card;` and line 153 `const zoneName = args.zone as string;`.
impacts.requiredChange: Rename all 10 .fromElements('card'|'targetZone',...) calls to .chooseElement (all are single-element picks). Redesign flipReorder's .chooseFrom<string> to .chooseElement<Zone> selecting a Zone element directly (string-choice form has no element-only replacement). If selection encoding is unified to element IDs, update execute bodies that currently consume resolved element objects (args.card as Card) to resolve IDs instead. UI already passes IDs via actionController.fill(name, card.id) so the UI side is mostly fine.

impacts.evidence: grep `from ['"]@?boardsmith` shows only canonical paths: App.vue:14 'boardsmith/ui', GameTable.vue:26/36 'boardsmith/ui', elements.ts:8 'boardsmith', flow.ts:9 'boardsmith', actions.ts:14 'boardsmith', game.ts:9 'boardsmith', tests/game.test.ts:2 'boardsmith/testing'. No '@boardsmith/*' imports. package.json dependency is `"boardsmith": "file:../../BoardSmith"`. No vendor dir or vite alias.

src/rules is sandbox-clean: grep for Math.random/Date.now/fetch/setTimeout/etc in src/rules returned nothing; game.ts shuffles via engine RNG (`Math.floor(this.random() * (i + 1))`) which is deterministic. However src/ui/components/GameTable.vue:90 `const timer = setTimeout(resolve, 5000);` (inside the demo animation-event handler) and GameTable.vue:263 `id: \`fly-stat-${cardId}-${Date.now()}\`` use host APIs.
impacts.requiredChange: No rules changes needed. If the expanded `boardsmith validate` / wired eslint sandbox rules scan all of src/ (not just src/rules), the two UI-only uses (setTimeout for animation timing, Date.now for a unique DOM key) may be flagged and need allow-listing or replacement (e.g. a counter instead of Date.now). UI animation timing is legitimate, so likely a lint-config/exception decision rather than a behavioral fix.

impacts.evidence: DemoGame declares only element collections (zoneA..zoneD: Zone, game.ts:35-41) and getter-computed arrays (faceUpZones/faceDownZones/allZones return fresh arrays). DemoPlayer.score is a number (game.ts). Card has suit/rank/faceUp (string/string/boolean). No plain array/object instance properties holding mutable game state; allCards in the constructor is a local variable, not a class property.

No game/element state holds class instances, Map, Set, functions, or circular/deeply-nested objects. The only Map (previousStatCardIds, GameTable.vue:302) is UI ref state, not game/element state. Card/Zone/Player fields are primitives.

grep for useBoardInteraction/injectBoardInteraction in src/ returned no matches. GameTable.vue uses useAnimationEvents() with optional chaining (line 157 `animationEvents?.registerHandler`), but that is a different composable, not useBoardInteraction; the component renders only inside <GameShell> via App.vue's #game-board slot.

Cards are matched by id everywhere (boardRef `{ id: card.id }` at actions.ts:30 etc; UI fill uses card.id). Zones are matched by name (actions.ts dragDrop boardRef `{ name: zone.name }` ~line 287; GameTable.vue findElement({ name: 'zone-a' }) lines 190-193; drop({ name: 'zone-a' })). Zone names ('zone-a'..'zone-d') are unique and there are no notation values, so precedence-based matching (id>notation>name) still resolves name-only refs correctly with no collision.

src/rules/actions.ts contains ~18 casts inside execute bodies (e.g. line 44 `args.card as Card`, line 45 `card.parent as Zone`, line 153 `args.zone as string`, flyToStat `ctx.player as DemoPlayer`). No `(ctx.game as` casts found (game is captured via closure, not ctx). These casts on selection args become unnecessary once execute() args are typed from declared selections.
impacts.requiredChange: Optional cleanup: remove now-redundant `args.x as T` casts once selection typing is generic. Low risk of new compile errors since the casts narrow from unknown/element to the correct concrete types; `args.zone as string` would only break if the redesigned flipReorder selection no longer yields a string.

impacts.evidence: grep for boardsmith/client, GameConnection, MeepleClient, new WebSocket in src/ and tests/ returned nothing. App.vue uses GameShell (boardsmith/ui) and the game runs on the CLI dev server (package.json `"dev": "npx boardsmith dev"`). No custom server/client connection wiring.

grep for debug/rewind/deck-stack/stackDeck/fullHistory in src/ and tests/ returned no matches. The only shuffle is the engine API `zone.shuffle()` (actions.ts flipReorder execute) and constructor RNG shuffle — not a debug/deck-stacking client endpoint. Tests (tests/game.test.ts) only use createTestGame assertions.

games.overall: This game will be impacted almost entirely by one fix: F23+F28 (selection-builder consolidation). Its five actions lean heavily on chooseFrom and fromElements (6 call sites), and chooseFrom is used not only for elements but for arbitrary non-element choices (player position via playerChoices, and suit strings). If chooseFrom/fromElements are removed in favor of chooseElement/chooseElements with ID-only encoding, this game must be substantially rewritten — including its execute() reads (args.card as Card, args.targetPlayer as number) and its two tests that pass { value, display } choice objects. That is the single biggest required change and is moderate effort. Every other audited fix is unaffected: imports are already canonical (boardsmith / boardsmith/ui / boardsmith/testing), there are no sandbox/determinism violations (rules use game.random() and deck.shuffle()), no plain mutable array/object game state, no non-serializable state, no useBoardInteraction usage (it consumes actionController via GameShell slot props), all board refs use id, no custom client/server wiring, and no debug-endpoint usage. F19+F25 is a minor optional cleanup of now-redundant casts.
games.usesBoardsmithHow: Consumes BoardSmith as a normal local file dependency: package.json has "boardsmith": "file:../../BoardSmith". All imports use canonical entry points only — 'boardsmith' (engine: Game, Player, Action, loop, eachPlayer, actionStep, Card/Hand/Deck/Space), 'boardsmith/ui' (GameShell, AutoUI, useFlyingElements, find* helpers, UseActionControllerReturn type), and 'boardsmith/testing' (createTestGame). No @boardsmith/* scoped paths, no vendored copy, no vite alias. Ships a custom UI (App.vue + GameTable.vue) layered over GameShell/AutoUI but no custom server/client; runs via the CLI dev server (npx boardsmith dev).

impacts.evidence: src/rules/actions.ts:54 ".fromElements<Card>('card', {", :92 ".fromElements<Card>('myCard', {", :155 ".fromElements<Card>('card', {"; :101 ".chooseFrom('targetPlayer', {", :164 ".chooseFrom('recipient', {", :206 ".chooseFrom<string>('suit', {". chooseFrom is also used for NON-element choices: :103 "choices: (ctx) => game.playerChoices({ excludeSelf: true, currentPlayer: ctx.player })" and :208 suit strings. Execute reads assume non-ID encoding: actions.ts:122 "const targetPosition = args.targetPlayer as number;" and tests/game.test.ts:35 "targetPlayer: { value: 2, display: 'Player 2' }".
impacts.requiredChange: Migrate the 3 fromElements card pickers to chooseElement (boardRef already returns {id}, so element encoding is close). Replace the 3 chooseFrom usages: the suit and player-position choices are not element selections, so they need whatever general/value-choice API survives the consolidation (or a chooseElement over the target Hand element + reading its owner). Update execute() to read the new ID/value encoding and update the two trade/gift tests that send { value, display } objects.

impacts.evidence: grep for GameConnection|MeepleClient|boardsmith/client|boardsmith/server returned nothing in src+tests. The game uses only GameShell/AutoUI (App.vue:17,22) and the CLI dev server (package.json scripts: "dev": "npx boardsmith dev"). No custom connect/action wiring.

games.overall: Go Fish will be affected primarily and heavily by F23+F28 (selection-builder consolidation). The game's single action "ask" is built entirely on .chooseFrom for a player target (number) and a rank (string) — neither of which are game elements — and the value encoding it relies on is the chooseFrom choice-object/scalar shape, not element IDs. That encoding leaks into rules (execute reads args.target as number / args.rank as string), the custom UI (handleCardClick/handleOpponentClick/getChoices/fill with {value,display}), and both test files (simulateAction with target:{value,display}). If chooseFrom is removed or its values forced to element IDs, the ask action cannot express a string-rank choice and needs a redesign — this is the single biggest required change and the only structural one. Everything else is minor: imports are already canonical (F27 clean), no determinism violations in src/rules (only a stray setTimeout in src/ui for animation), no declared array/object or non-serializable state, no custom server/client, and board refs already use IDs.
games.usesBoardsmithHow: Consumes BoardSmith as a normal "boardsmith": "file:../../BoardSmith" dependency (node_modules/boardsmith is a symlink to the repo). No vendoring, no vite/vitest alias. All imports use canonical entry points: 'boardsmith', 'boardsmith/ui', 'boardsmith/ai', 'boardsmith/testing'. Ships a custom Vue UI (App.vue + GameTable.vue inside GameShell) alongside AutoUI, plus AI hooks (src/rules/ai.ts). Runs via the CLI dev server (npm run dev = npx boardsmith dev); no custom server/client wiring. .boardsmith/rules-bundle.mjs is a generated build artifact, not vendored source.

impacts.evidence: src/rules/actions.ts:35 ".chooseFrom('target', {" and :48 ".chooseFrom<string>('rank', {" — both are generic value choices (player position number, rank string), NOT element selections. Value encoding is leaked everywhere: actions.ts:80-83 "const targetPosition = args.target as number" / "const rank = args.rank as string"; UI src/ui/components/GameTable.vue:308-356 (isSelectingPlayer/isSelectingRank treat picks as type==='choice', handleOpponentClick fills 'target' with playerChoice.value {value,display}, handleCardClick fills 'rank' with a string, isOpponentHandSelectable reads (c.value as any)?.value===position); tests/complete-game.test.ts:49-51,175-176,235-236 "target: { value: opponentPosition, display: opponent.name }, rank: rankToAsk".
impacts.requiredChange: If chooseFrom is removed/aliased and selection values are unified to element IDs, the 'ask' action must be redesigned: a rank is a string and a target is a player, neither maps to an element ID. Either chooseFrom must be preserved for non-element value choices, or the action must be reworked (e.g. choose a representative card element for rank, choose the opponent's Hand element for target) and the rules/UI/tests must be rewritten to read element IDs. At minimum a rename if chooseFrom is aliased.

games.overall: Hex will be very lightly affected by the planned fixes. It is one of the simplest reference games: a single ID-based chooseElement action, canonical 'boardsmith/*' imports everywhere, no custom server/client, no debug endpoints, no plain-array/object state, no Maps/Sets/class-instances persisted (winner is a Player ref handled by the serializer), and no sandbox violations in src/rules. The only real touch-points are cosmetic: a now-dead `if (!boardInteraction)` null-check that the F21 throw-on-misuse change makes redundant (still in-shell, won't break), and a handful of now-unnecessary `as` casts in actions/AI that F19+F25 typing would let you drop. The single biggest (still trivial) item is verifying that chooseElement execute args keep resolving to the Cell element instance under the F23+F28 selection consolidation, since actions.ts and ai.ts both depend on that resolution (execute reads `args.cell as Cell`, AI reads `move.args.cell as number`/the raw ID).
games.usesBoardsmithHow: Imports the library exclusively via canonical subpath entry points: 'boardsmith' (engine), 'boardsmith/ui', 'boardsmith/ai', 'boardsmith/testing'. package.json declares dependency "boardsmith": "file:../../BoardSmith" and node_modules/boardsmith is a symlink to /Users/jtsmith/BoardSmith — NOT vendored, NOT aliased (vite.config.ts has no alias). Ships a custom Vue UI (App.vue + HexBoard.vue) inside GameShell plus the AutoUI comparison panel. Uses the CLI dev server (npx boardsmith dev) only; no custom server or client connection wiring. AI hooks wired through gameDefinition.ai in src/rules/index.ts.

impacts.evidence: Only canonical selection builder is used: src/rules/actions.ts:12 `.chooseElement<Cell>('cell', {...})`. Grep for chooseFrom/fromElements/chooseElements returned no other hits. Value encoding is already ID-based: src/ui/components/HexBoard.vue:119 `await props.actionController.execute('placeStone', { cell: cell.id })`; AI reads IDs at src/rules/ai.ts:389 `const cellId = move.args.cell as number`. Execute resolves to the element at src/rules/actions.ts:31 `const cell = args.cell as Cell`.
impacts.requiredChange: None required since chooseElement is the canonical method being kept. Caveat to confirm during the consolidation: execute must continue resolving args.cell to the Cell instance (actions.ts:31) while the wire/selection value stays the numeric id (HexBoard.vue:119, ai.ts:389). If consolidation changed execute args to raw IDs, actions.ts:31 cast would break.

No designer-declared plain array/object mutable state. HexGame fields: `boardSize: number` (game.ts:29), `board!: Board` (element, game.ts:32), `winner?: HexPlayer` (player ref, game.ts:35). HexPlayer.stonesPlaced: number (elements.ts:191). Board.boardSize: number, Cell.q/r: number. All primitives or element/collection references; no Proxy-wrapped arrays/objects.

Maps/Sets in src/rules/ai.ts are local function variables only, never assigned to element fields. The one non-primitive stored, `winner?: HexPlayer` (game.ts:35), is a Player and is handled by the serializer via __playerRef (verified /Users/jtsmith/BoardSmith/src/engine/utils/serializer.ts:37-40 `if (value instanceof Player) return { __playerRef: player.seat }`), so it will not trip the new fail-loud path.

src/ui/components/HexBoard.vue:67 `const boardInteraction = useBoardInteraction();` then :101 `if (!boardInteraction) return false;`. The null-check relies on the old undefined fallback but is dead in-shell, not load-bearing.

No name-only or notation-only matching that could collide.

AI helpers cast game repeatedly, e.g. src/rules/ai.ts:349 `const hexGame = game as HexGame;` and read `move.args.cell as number` (ai.ts:389, 424, 461, 713, 907). impacts.evidence: No custom server/client wiring. Grep found no boardsmith/client, GameConnection, or MeepleClient imports. Connection is handled entirely by <GameShell> (src/ui/App.vue:30) over the CLI dev server (package.json scripts: "dev": "npx boardsmith dev"). actionController comes from the shell slot, not a hand-built connection.

No debug/rewind/deck-stack/shuffle/full-history client calls in src/ui or tests. tests/game.test.ts uses only in-process testing helpers (createTestGame, toDebugString, traceAction, logAvailableActions, diffSnapshots from 'boardsmith/testing'); these are local utilities, not debug HTTP endpoints.

games.overall: Moderately affected, driven almost entirely by one fix. The game's imports, sandbox-cleanliness, serialization, server wiring, board-ref matching, and debug usage are all clean. The single biggest required change is F23+F28: the game leans heavily on the action-builder's chooseFrom() (6 call sites in src/rules/actions.ts), which the audit plans to remove. Five of those are legitimate scalar string choices (operation/adjustment/track/newValue/keepDrafted) that need a replacement primitive, and the draft action uses a NON-ID string encoding ("d10:0"/"d10:10") to smuggle the D10 0-or-10 choice through the selection layer — that pattern cannot map cleanly to "selection values are always element IDs" and will need real rework. Secondary, lower-effort exposure: F3 (settings-shadow arrays + plain mutable player arrays) and F19+F25 (pervasive ctx.game/args casts to clean up).
games.usesBoardsmithHow: Consumes BoardSmith as a local file dependency ("boardsmith": "file:../../BoardSmith" in package.json) — no vendoring, no vite alias, no @boardsmith/* scoped paths. All imports use the canonical entry points: 'boardsmith' (engine), 'boardsmith/ui' (GameShell, AutoUI, Die3D, useFLIP, injectBoardInteraction), 'boardsmith/testing'. Ships a Custom UI (src/ui/App.vue → GameTable/DiceShelf/ScoreSheet) alongside AutoUI, both inside <GameShell>. Runs via the CLI dev server (npx boardsmith dev); no custom server or client connection wiring.

impacts.evidence: src/rules/actions.ts uses chooseFrom 6x: line 20 .chooseFrom<string>('die', ...) (draft), line 99 'operation', line 119 'adjustment', line 169 'track', line 382 'newValue', line 450 'keepDrafted'. The draft action encodes element selection as a NON-ID string with a value suffix — actions.ts:30-32 'choices.push(`${die.name}:0`); choices.push(`${die.name}:10`)' and execute parses it back: actions.ts:62-65 'const parts = choice.split(":"); const dieName = parts[0]; const overrideValue = parts[1] ? parseInt(parts[1],10):undefined'. chooseElement is used 3x (die1/die2/die at actions.ts:263,274,368) and would survive, but every chooseFrom site breaks if chooseFrom is removed/aliased.
impacts.requiredChange: Replace all 6 chooseFrom() calls. The 5 scalar string choices (operation/adjustment/track/newValue/keepDrafted) need a kept scalar primitive (e.g. enterText/number or whatever replaces chooseFrom for non-element values). The draft 'die' selection must be re-modeled as an element selection (chooseElement/fromElements with IDs); the D10 0-or-10 choice can no longer ride on a string suffix and needs a separate scalar selection or post-draft step. Reading args.die as a parsed string also goes away.

impacts.evidence: rg of all imports shows only canonical paths: src/rules/game.ts:1 "from 'boardsmith'", src/rules/actions.ts:1, src/rules/elements.ts:10-11, src/rules/flow.ts:9, src/ui/App.vue:2 "from 'boardsmith/ui'", src/ui/components/GameTable.vue:5 'boardsmith/ui', src/ui/components/DiceShelf.vue:3 'boardsmith/ui', tests/game.test.ts:8 'boardsmith/testing'. No '@boardsmith/*' imports. package.json: "boardsmith": "file:../../BoardSmith". No vendoring/alias.

Game stores array state in a manual settings shadow: src/rules/game.ts:70-76 get/set draftedDice via 'this.settings._draftedDiceIds' and game.ts:79-84 draftedValues via 'this.settings._draftedValues' (comment line 67: 'HMR-safe state (stored in settings)'). impacts.requiredChange: If the engine stops Proxy-wrapping declared arrays / changes settings-shadow persistence, verify draftedDice/draftedValues still round-trip and the player's in-place mutations of ingredientTracks/potionsCrafted still persist across save/HMR; may need to move these to a serializable shape or element children.

impacts.evidence: All class-instance state lives behind underscore-prefixed private fields, which base toJSON skips: game-element.ts:705 '!key.startsWith("_")'. (args.die1 as any).id : args.die1 as number' (also :396). Same 'ctx.game as PolyPotionsGame' pattern throughout flow.ts (e.g. flow.ts:61,100,111,127).

impacts.evidence: No custom connection/server wiring: grep for GameConnection|MeepleClient|boardsmith/client|new WebSocket|connect( across src and tests returned zero hits. package.json scripts use 'npx boardsmith dev' (CLI dev server); UI relies on GameShell defaults (App.vue:56); tests use boardsmith/testing createTestGame.

No debug/rewind/deck-stack/shuffle/history HTTP endpoint usage. The only 'debug' references are the boardsmith/testing helper toDebugString (tests/game.test.ts:4,241) and a seed string 'debug-demo' (test:237) — pure test-time utilities, not gated server endpoints. No debug usage in src/ui (GameTable.vue/ScoreSheet.vue/DiceShelf.vue clean).

games.overall: MERC will be heavily impacted, dominated by F23+F28. The game's entire action layer is built on the soon-to-be-removed builders: 90 .chooseFrom() and 2 .fromElements() call sites across the six files in src/rules/actions, plus 25 .chooseElement() calls whose results are decoded by asSector/asSquad/asCombatantModel helpers that assume the arg is a resolved element INSTANCE (not an ID). Unifying selection encoding to element IDs and removing chooseFrom/fromElements is a large, cross-cutting rewrite — the single biggest required change. Secondary impacts: a determinism violation (Date.now in a rules debug block), heavy reliance on a this.settings shadow store plus many declared plain object/array state fields (F3), and Set/Map fields in game state (F7) that fail-loud serialization may newly reject. Import paths are clean (canonical boardsmith/... form) and the game ships no custom server/client or debug-endpoint usage, so F27/F8-14/F12/F21/F22 are largely unaffected.
games.usesBoardsmithHow: Imports via the canonical bare-specifier form only ('boardsmith', 'boardsmith/ui', 'boardsmith/session', 'boardsmith/ai', 'boardsmith/runtime', 'boardsmith/testing', 'boardsmith/utils'); zero '@boardsmith/...' imports (the one '@boardsmith' hit is a comment in src/rules/elements.ts:1536). However it does NOT consume the live workspace package — package.json pins a vendored tarball: "boardsmith": "file:./vendor/boardsmith-0.0.1-undofix.tgz" with a matching overrides entry, and vendor/ holds dozens of dated tgz builds. So all audit fixes only reach this game when the vendored tgz is rebuilt/updated. Ships a custom UI (src/ui App.vue + ~40 Vue components) and custom AI helpers, but no custom server/client wiring — runs on the CLI dev server + GameShell defaults. Uses boardsmith/utils (easing fns) and boardsmith/runtime subpaths, which must remain exported by the new build.

impacts.evidence: 90 .chooseFrom() + 2 .fromElements() call sites across src/rules/actions/. e.g. src/rules/actions/rebel-movement.ts:169 '.chooseFrom<number>(\'militiaCount\', {', :526 '.chooseFrom<\'primary\' | \'secondary\'>(\'squadType\'', :784 '.chooseFrom<string>(\'combatantName\'); src/rules/actions/rebel-equipment.ts:574 '.fromElements<CombatantModel>(\'actingMerc\'), :582 '.fromElements<Equipment>(\'equipment\'); plus dictator-actions.ts (~50 chooseFrom), day-one-actions.ts, rebel-combat.ts, rebel-economy.ts. Also 25 .chooseElement() (e.g. rebel-movement.ts:125 '.chooseElement<Sector>(\'destination\')) whose results are decoded by INSTANCE-assuming helpers: src/rules/actions/helpers.ts:381 'export function asSector(element: unknown){ if (element instanceof Sector) return element; ...throw }' (also asSquad:393, asCombatantModel:369), called 27x. Many chooseFrom values are primitives (numbers/strings/yes-no like rebel-economy.ts:1342 \'confirm\'), not element IDs.
impacts.requiredChange: Migrate all 92 chooseFrom/fromElements call sites to the canonical chooseElement/chooseElements (for element selections) and to whatever primitive-choice replacement the library keeps (for the many string/number/enum choices). Rewrite the asSector/asSquad/asCombatantModel decoders (and all 27 call sites) to resolve element IDs via game.getElementById, since unified ID encoding means execute args arrive as numeric IDs rather than element instances.

The single '@boardsmith' string is a code comment: src/rules/elements.ts:1536. package.json depends on "boardsmith": file:./vendor/...tgz.
impacts.requiredChange: None for import paths. Caveat: confirm the new boardsmith build still exports the 'boardsmith/utils' and 'boardsmith/runtime' subpaths the game imports, and rebuild the vendored tgz for any fix to apply.

impacts.evidence: RULES (determinism violation): src/rules/actions/dictator-actions.ts:86-87 'if (!g.__tacticsDbgAt || Date.now() - g.__tacticsDbgAt > 1500) { g.__tacticsDbgAt = Date.now();' inside a [TACTICS-DEBUG] throttle. No Math.random/timers/network found in src/rules otherwise. UI (would newly fail expanded validate/lint): src/ui/components/MapGrid.vue:351 'id: `death-${Date.now()}-...`', src/ui/components/AssignToSquadPanel.vue:123 'return id ? String(id) : Math.random().toString();', and 20 setTimeout uses across src/ui (animations). No eslint config present in the game (.eslintrc*/eslint.config.* absent).
impacts.requiredChange: Remove the Date.now() debug throttle in dictator-actions.ts (use engine RNG/clock or just drop debug). For UI, replace Date.now()/Math.random() id-generation with a deterministic counter and confirm setTimeout animation usage is permitted by the expanded validate scan (or whitelist src/ui). Add the scaffolded eslint sandbox config.

impacts.evidence: Heavy this.settings shadow store in src/rules/game.ts:764-795 (combatantData/equipmentData/sectorData/tacticsData/setupConfigurations getters+setters via '(this.settings as Record<string, unknown>).combatantData = data'), and game.ts:740 'const settings = this.settings as Record<string, unknown>'. Plus many declared plain object/array state fields mutated at runtime: game.ts:597 pendingArtilleryAllocation object, :626 pendingGeneralissimoHire {drawnMercIds:number[]}, :636 pendingLockdownMilitia, :1498 declinedPlayers:[]. Note pendingLootMap (game.ts:721) already uses the correct this.persistentMap API.
impacts.requiredChange: Re-home settings-shadow data and mutable declared array/object state onto a supported serializable shape (element children or persistentMap/persistentArray-style APIs, as already done for pendingLootMap), since F3 stops Proxy-wrapping declared arrays and changes settings-shadow persistence. Verify save/HMR survival for each pending* field.

impacts.evidence: Set/Map held in game state: src/rules/game.ts:669 '_pinochetControlledSnapshot: Set<string> | null = null;' (public field), :595 'private _rebelActionCounts: Map<number, number> = new Map();' (commented 'private = ephemeral, not serialized'), and a Set in a state object type at game.ts:495 'beforeAttackHealingProcessed?: Set<string>'. Combatant arrays (combat-types.ts:14 'export interface Combatant') are plain data, fine.
impacts.requiredChange: If serializeValue reaches these fields, fail-loud serialization will throw on the Set/Map. Convert _pinochetControlledSnapshot (Set) and beforeAttackHealingProcessed to arrays, and ensure _rebelActionCounts (Map) is genuinely excluded from serialization or converted. Confirm whether private fields are serialized in the new engine.

impacts.evidence: src/ui/components/GameTable.vue:52 'const boardInteraction = useBoardInteraction();' — called inside a GameShell child; the identifier appears only at the declaration (no '?.' or 'if (boardInteraction)' null-check fallback). No injectBoardInteraction usage elsewhere.
impacts.requiredChange: None (correct in-shell usage; throwing behavior won't trigger).

impacts.evidence: No board-interaction ref/highlight/select calls matching elements by name or notation. Combat highlighting works via element IDs (src/ui/composables/useCombatSequence.ts:91-114 uses targetIds from roll events). Other 'highlight' hits are CSS classes/comments (e.g. AssignToSquadPanel.vue:499 '.subtitle.highlight').
impacts.requiredChange: None.

impacts.evidence: Casts in execute/condition bodies in src/rules: 8 'ctx.game as' (e.g. rebel-equipment.ts:512 'const g = ctx.game as MERCGame;', :563 'isNotInActiveCombat(ctx.game as MERCGame)'), 44 'ctx.player as', and 92 'args.<x> as' casts (used because chooseFrom returns untyped primitives that get cast before use).
impacts.requiredChange: Mostly cleanup once ctx.game is generic (ActionContext<G>) and execute args are typed from declared selections: many 'ctx.game as MERCGame' and 'args.x as' casts become unnecessary and a few wrong casts may newly fail to compile. Tightly coupled to the F23+F28 migration.

impacts.evidence: grep over src+tests for boardsmith/client, GameConnection, MeepleClient, new WebSocket, createServer/GameServer: zero matches. Game uses only the CLI dev server + GameShell; tests use boardsmith/testing (createTestGame) and boardsmith/runtime (GameRunner).
impacts.requiredChange: None.

impacts.evidence: grep over src/ui, src/rules, tests for rewind /debug deck-stack shuffle /history fullHistory debug endpoints: no client calls to those endpoints (only local debug-config.ts / console debug logging, which are unrelated).
